articles.title: UK: War shock weighs on growth outlook – Nomura
articles.expand: Nomura’s analysts see the Iran war keeping UK inflation above target until mid-2027 and weakening the 2026 growth outlook beyond Q1. UK GDP growth slowed to 0.1% q-o-q in both Q3 and Q4 2025.

articles.title: USD/CHF Price Forecast: Spot tests lower Bollinger band as bearish momentum builds
articles.expand: USD/CHF trades on the back foot on Friday and is set for a second straight weekly decline amid broad-based weakness in the US Dollar (USD). At the time of writing, the pair is trading around 0.7773, hovering near two-month lows.

articles.title: USD/CAD climbs after Canada jobs data surprises to the downside.
articles.expand: USD/CAD edges higher on Friday as softer-than-expected Canadian employment data weighs on the Canadian Dollar (CAD), even as the US Dollar (USD) remains on the back foot following mixed US labor market data and hopes for a US-Iran deal to end the war.

articles.title: Fed's Goolsbee: inflation has not been great. Job market is pretty much stable
articles.expand: The job market is pretty much stable. There is not a lot of evidence that job market is falling apart. Inflation has not been great and is going the wrong way. There is not a lot of evidence of the job market deterioration. Inflation rises not just energy, was elevated before war. We stop making progress on inflation last year In the last few month...

articles.title: ECB: War-driven inflation keeps hikes in focus – Nomura
articles.expand: Nomura’s George Buckley and team argue that even a swift resolution to the US-Iran war and reopening of the Strait would not necessarily stop the European Central Bank (ECB) from raising rates in June.

articles.title: Mixed feelings after the April Non-Farm Payrolls beat and Consumer Sentiment miss – Market Check

articles.expand: TD Securities strategists Robert Both and Emma Lawrence note that weaker Canadian employment in April, including job losses and a higher unemployment rate, is unlikely to bring the Bank of Canada (BoC) closer to rate cuts.

articles.title: Gold: PBoC buying underpins central bank demand – Commerzbank
articles.expand: Commerzbank’s Barbara Lambrecht reports that China’s central bank (PBoC) used a temporary Gold price dip in April to extend its buying streak to 18 months, with the largest addition since December 2024.

articles.title: United States Nonfarm Payrolls beat expectations: Why is the US Dollar weakening?
articles.expand: The United States (US) created 115K new jobs in April, much better than the 62K anticipated by markets. The unemployment rate in the same month was confirmed at 4.3%, as expected. The Nonfarm Payrolls (NFP) report came in better than anticipated, yet the US Dollar (USD) eased with the news.

articles.title: Markets Embrace Strong US Employment Report, but Iran Remains the Bigger Risk
articles.expand: The US jobs report gave stock markets exactly what they wanted — proof the economy is still holding up without reigniting fears of runaway inflation. articles.title: US Payrolls Beat Expectations With 115k Growth, But Wage Growth Stays Calm
articles.expand: US job growth slowed in April but remained significantly stronger than expectes. articles.title: Breaking: Nonfarm Payrolls rise by 115K in April vs. 62K forecast
articles.expand: Nonfarm Payrolls (NFP) in the United States (US) rose by 115K in April, the US Bureau of Labor Statistics (BLS) reported on Friday. This print followed the 185K increase (revised from 178K) recorded in March and surpassed the market expectation of 62K by a wide margin.
